summary: Drew DeVault's January 2022 status update covers rapid progress on his programming language's cryptography suite, standard library modules, and low-level systems work including a RISC-V kernel collaboration. The language now supports Argon2, ChaCha20, Poly1305, and high-level AEAD/key derivation utilities, with TLS support on the roadmap. He also highlights SourceHut's growth with two new full-time engineers and progress on GraphQL APIs and webhooks. The update reflects a productive period across both the language and SourceHut projects.
key_points: The language's cryptography suite has grown rapidly, with libsodium-style high-level utilities and a roadmap toward TLS support
Standard library additions include path manipulation, date/time, MIME types, and cmsg support for Wayland implementation
A collaborator is developing a RISC-V kernel in the language, demonstrating its viability for low-level systems programming
SourceHut hired two new full-time engineers, one funded by an NLNet grant, to accelerate GraphQL API development
The todo.sr.ht writable GraphQL API is nearly ready to ship after legacy webhook support and security review
money_quotes: I also implemented an efficient path manipulation module for the standard library (something I would really have liked to have in C!)
I also finished up cmsg support (for sendmsg and recvmsg), which is necessary for the Wayland implementation I'm working on (and was a major pain in the ass).

I spent some time working with another collaborator, who is developing a RISC-V kernel in our language, implementing a serial driver for the SiFive UART, plus improving the device tree loader and UEFI support.
Perhaps enough to implement an SSH client?
One of the standard library contributors also wrote a side-project to implement Ray Tracing in One Weekend in our language
key_insight: The programming language is maturing rapidly with production-grade cryptography, systems-level capabilities demonstrated by a RISC-V kernel, and growing contributor momentum, while SourceHut expands its team to ship GraphQL APIs.
